patterns: EC2 runs Python 3.9 — use Optional[X] and List[X] from typing, not X | None or list[X]
from __future__ import annotations breaks SQLAlchemy model evaluation on Python 3.9
Use async/await patterns with FastAPI and SQLAlchemy async sessions
Content-hash deduplication (SHA-256 of key fields) prevents duplicate database entries
APScheduler AsyncIOScheduler integrates cleanly with FastAPI lifespan events
Pydantic v2 BaseSettings handles .env loading with env_prefix for namespacing
Sub-agents work well for parallelizing independent component development
gotchas: pkill -f can kill parent shell — use kill $(pgrep ...) instead
nohup may not create log files if working directory changes — use absolute paths
Insurance quote APIs all require partner agreements — no free public APIs exist
SQLAlchemy 2.0 Mapped[] annotations require proper typing imports on Python 3.9
conventions: Commit format: 'clawdbot: Description' for Clawdbot changes, 'feat: [Story ID] - Title' for Ralph
Project structure: projects/<name>/src/ with subdirectories for api, models, scrapers, etc.
FastAPI routes: separate router files per domain, mounted with prefix in main.py
Always run syntax check (ast.parse) on all Python files before committing
